stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I don’t care about making popular decisions to win elections, but about standing up for my principles and values.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I don’t care about making popular decisions to win elections, but about standing up for my principles and values.”
